question_text: Task assigned with a two-day deadline.
option_a: I plan milestones, start early, and deliver on time.
option_b: I procrastinate and risk missing the deadline.

question_text: Complex task with unclear steps.
option_a: I break it into subtasks and track progress visibly.
option_b: I start haphazardly without a plan.

question_text: Several messages arrive with different deadlines and levels of urgency.
option_a: I sort them by urgency, record the deadlines, and schedule when to respond.
option_b: I answer whichever message catches my attention and forget the rest.

question_text: An experiment may need to be reproduced months after I finish it.
option_a: I rely on memory and discard intermediate settings once I see a result.
option_b: I record parameters, versions, observations, and deviations in a reproducible log.

question_text: I have an appointment across town immediately after another commitment.
option_a: I check travel time, allow a buffer, and prepare what I need in advance.
option_b: I leave planning until the last minute and assume the timing will work out.

question_text: A small code change appears to work in the example I tried manually.
option_a: I merge it immediately without recording or testing related behavior.
option_b: I add focused tests, review the diff, and document the behavior before merging.

question_text: A supply cabinet must be counted before an order is placed.
option_a: I count systematically, resolve discrepancies, and save the final inventory.
option_b: I estimate by sight and place the order without checking quantities.

question_text: An application form requires information from several separate records.
option_a: I enter guesses for missing fields so I can submit it quickly.
option_b: I gather the records, verify each field, and review the form before submission.

question_text: A long project has no external update scheduled for several weeks.
option_a: I set internal milestones and review progress regularly instead of waiting.
option_b: I stop tracking progress until someone asks for a final update.

question_text: An unexpected outage occurs near the end of my work shift.
option_a: I leave without recording what happened because my scheduled time is over.
option_b: I document the state, notify the responsible people, and provide a clear handoff.

question_text: I promise to send someone a resource after a conversation later this week.
option_a: I create a reminder and send the resource by the time I promised.
option_b: I assume I will remember and make no note of the commitment.

question_text: A planning meeting ends with several decisions but no assigned owners.
option_a: I leave without notes and expect people to remember what they volunteered for.
option_b: I summarize decisions, confirm owners and dates, and circulate the action list.

question_text: A routine maintenance task includes a safety procedure that takes extra time.
option_a: I follow each safety step and record completion even when no one is watching.
option_b: I skip the inconvenient steps because the task usually finishes without problems.

question_text: Several people are editing related documents that have similar file names.
option_a: I save copies wherever convenient and depend on timestamps to find the right version.
option_b: I use agreed names, maintain version history, and archive obsolete copies clearly.

question_text: A recurring payment changes amount after a service plan is updated.
option_a: I update the record, verify the next charge, and schedule a review if needed.
option_b: I ignore the change and hope future charges are correct.

question_text: A report depends on claims collected from sources of uneven reliability.
option_a: I include every claim without tracking where it came from.
option_b: I trace each claim to its source, assess reliability, and mark unresolved evidence.

question_text: A deliverable is finished several hours before it is due.
option_a: I use the remaining time to review requirements, correct errors, and verify the final package.
option_b: I submit it immediately without checking because finishing early is enough.

question_text: I must learn a new procedure before using it on an important task tomorrow.
option_a: I skim the first section and improvise the rest during the important task.
option_b: I study the procedure, practice the difficult steps, and note questions before tomorrow.

question_text: A research folder contains useful excerpts but inconsistent citation details.
option_a: I complete the citation records and link each excerpt to its original source.
option_b: I keep the excerpts without sources and assume I can reconstruct citations later.

question_text: I am interrupted halfway through a delicate multi-step task.
option_a: I restart at an arbitrary point after the interruption and trust that I missed nothing.
option_b: I mark the completed step, secure the work, and resume from a verified point afterward.
